recommended_external_components.snippet: 8.2.2.1.5 Input Capacitor Selection The minimum input capacitance required is given in 方程式 9. OUT OUT IN(min) INripple IN SW I ×V C = V ×V ×F (9) TI recommends using a high quality X5R or X7R input de
For adjustable output voltage, keep the lower leg from Table 7-2 and change only the upper feedback resistor using Equation 6.
Use Table 7-2 inductor values instead of a generic ripple-current heuristic when selecting operating points for TPS56C215.
Use the Table 7-2 output-capacitance window for D-CAP3 stability and transient performance.

component_value_hints.snippet: Typical 1.2-V, 12-A application schematic shows the reference inductor, capacitor bank, VREG5 bypass, and MODE divider values.

layout_hints.hint: 10 Layout 10.1 Layout Guidelines • Recommend a four-layer or six-layer PCB for good thermal performance and with maximum ground plane. 3" x 3", four-layer PCB with 2-oz. copper used as example. • Rec

topology_hints.hint: 8 Application and Implementation Note Information in the following applications sections is not part of the TI component specification, and TI does not warrant its accuracy or completeness. TI’s cust

design_recommendations.rule: MODE must be derived from the VREG5 rail through a resistor divider to AGND, is sampled during startup, and only resets after VIN power cycling.

design_recommendations.note: Latest TI datasheet examples in Rev. H use a 10-kOhm lower feedback resistor across the Table 7-2 operating points.

design_formulas.name: RUPPER_feedback_divider_bias
design_formulas.purpose: Latest TI datasheet examples in Rev. H use a 10-kOhm lower feedback resistor across the Table 7-2 operating points.

components.purpose: 8.2.2.1.5 Input Capacitor Selection The minimum input capacitance required is given in 方程式 9. OUT OUT IN(min) INripple IN SW I ×V C = V ×V ×F (9) TI recommends using a high quality X5R or X7R input de
For adjustable output voltage, keep the lower leg from Table 7-2 and change only the upper feedback resistor using Equation 6.
Use Table 7-2 inductor values instead of a generic ripple-current heuristic when selecting operating points for TPS56C215.
Use the Table 7-2 output-capacitance window for D-CAP3 stability and transient performance.

application_notes.title: Mode Pin Configuration
application_notes.content: MODE must be derived from the VREG5 rail through a resistor divider to AGND, is sampled during startup, and only resets after VIN power cycling.

application_notes.title: Feedback Divider Bias
application_notes.content: Latest TI datasheet examples in Rev. H use a 10-kOhm lower feedback resistor across the Table 7-2 operating points.

application_notes.title: Layout Guidance
application_notes.content: 10 Layout 10.1 Layout Guidelines • Recommend a four-layer or six-layer PCB for good thermal performance and with maximum ground plane. 3" x 3", four-layer PCB with 2-oz. copper used as example. • Rec
